message:
feat(daemon): add background embedding job support via Unix socket

Extends the xf daemon to support submitting, monitoring, and canceling
long-running embedding jobs through the existing Unix socket protocol.

New Protocol Messages (src/daemon/protocol.rs):
- SubmitEmbeddingJob: Submit background embedding with parameters:
  - db_path, index_path, model_id
  - two_tier mode with fast_model/quality_model options
- EmbeddingJobStatus: Query job status by db_path or get all jobs
- CancelEmbeddingJob: Cancel jobs by db_path and optional model_id
- JobSubmitted, JobStatus, JobCancelled response types
- EmbeddingJobInfo struct for job metadata

Daemon Core (src/daemon/core.rs):
- Job queue management with priority scheduling
- Progress tracking and status reporting
- Graceful cancellation with cleanup

Client (src/daemon/client.rs):
- Helper methods for submitting and monitoring jobs
- Async job status polling

CLI (src/cli.rs, src/main.rs):
- Integration with daemon for background embedding
- Fallback to direct embedding when daemon unavailable

Storage (src/storage.rs):
- Job persistence for crash recovery
- Status tracking across daemon restarts

This enables long-running embedding operations to continue even after
the CLI exits, with progress queryable through subsequent commands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -176,6 +176,20 @@ pub struct IndexArgs {
     /// to enable `--mode two-tier` search with score blending.
     #[arg(long)]
     pub two_tier: bool,
+
+    /// Skip embedding generation entirely.
+    ///
+    /// Useful for quick indexing when only lexical search is needed.
+    /// You can generate embeddings later with `xf daemon` background jobs.
+    #[arg(long)]
+    pub no_embeddings: bool,
+
+    /// Force synchronous embedding (block until complete).
+    ///
+    /// By default, embeddings are generated in the background via the daemon.
+    /// Use this flag to wait for embedding completion before returning.
+    #[arg(long)]
+    pub sync_embeddings: bool,
 }
 
 #[derive(Args, Debug)]
